judgment.summary: Profitable company with strong cash flow and a low valuation, but facing declining earnings growth and analyst estimates.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 5.6%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Trailing P/E 8.33x and Forward P/E 7.68x suggest a very low valuation.
PEG ratio 0.81 indicates potential undervaluation relative to earnings growth.
Strong Free Cash Flow (TTM) of $4.08B supports operations and shareholder returns.
Return on Equity 25.1% is robust, indicating efficient use of shareholder capital.
Gross margin 40.9% and Operating margin 18.0% show healthy operational profitability.
negatives: Earnings growth YoY is negative at -6.2%, and quarterly earnings growth is -13.5%.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year are slightly negative.
Total debt $11.67B exceeds total cash $9.34B, though manageable with strong FCF.
Quick ratio 0.22 is low, suggesting reliance on inventory or slower-moving assets to meet short-term obligations.
Analyst consensus is 'hold' with a wide dispersion in price targets.

news.paragraph: PayPal Holdings (PYPL) is seeing new attention as activist investor Michael Burry has initiated a new position in the company. While headlines are dominated by news of SpaceX's upcoming IPO, Burry's stake suggests a belief in PayPal's underlying value or potential for operational improvement. Traders should monitor any further commentary from Burry's firm or management's response to this development.

headlines.title: Factbox-IPO-bound SpaceX's board is stacked with Musk empire loyalists
SpaceX IPO Set to Deliver Massive Returns to Investors
PayPal Holdings Inc. (PYPL): Michael Burry Initiates New Position

examples.body: We shorted the market in my private community and every member killed it. We are currently trading with my new AI trading bot! $ETH.X $SOL.X $PYPL
$PYPL I never got the split shares 😢 $SPY
$PYPL What you cant see from that speedo graphic is that the needle has actually done a full anticlockwise revolution before landing where it did
